__doc__: Single source of truth for the harness's hard-gate vocabulary and component inventory. Machine-read by scripts/check_manifest.py (CI). Edit HERE — the copies in skills/feature-intake, rules/auto-correct-scope.md, and hooks/risk-corroboration.sh must not diverge (the checker enforces the hook match). JSON (not YAML) so the checker needs only the Python stdlib.

hard_gates.__doc__: detectable = diff-scannable by risk-corroboration.sh (must match its add_cat + category_mode exactly). judgment = Rule-4 STOP items that cannot be regex-detected (human/agent judgment only).

detectable.desc: login, logout, sessions, JWT, password, refresh/oauth token, cookies
roles, permissions, tenant/company scope, access control
provider SDKs, webhooks, and dependency manifests (see notes.external_provider_vs_rule3)
API route/method, response envelope, client-visible behavior
settings.json, hooks/*, .claude/hooks/*, a core skill engine (render_plan.py)

judgment.desc: removing existing behavior, even if seemingly unused (not regex-detectable)
request-scoped <-> isolated/background session or transaction scope change
swapping a service/pattern (cache impl, shared data-access base)

contracts.__doc__: contract surface->consumers map for Q3 blast-radius; read by scripts/check-contract-impact.sh, validated by scripts/check_manifest.py.

notes.external_provider_vs_rule3: A dependency-manifest edit (requirements*.txt / package.json / pyproject.toml / go.mod / Gemfile) trips external-provider, but auto-correct-scope Rule 3 authorizes auto-adding a dependency. Resolution: a pure dependency bump runs in the high-risk lane OR is recorded as a Rule-3 deviation in SUMMARY. The gate stays broad by design — over-flagging a dependency change is safer than missing a new SDK integration.
